equivalence_argument: ExecutorRegistry can deactivate an address, but there is no TeeDA log handler that validates a deactivation owner topic and calls it.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: No audited repo handles ExecutorDeactivated logs by extracting topic[1], deactivating that owner, and rejecting missing owner topics as InvalidEventData.
related_but_insufficient_code: ExecutorRegistry::deactivate_executor marks an existing entry inactive, but it is not connected to any log event parser in the audited repos.
VerificationError::InvalidEventData exists, but no deactivation log topic-count validation path was found.

rationale: The new deactivation handler validates topic count, extracts the indexed owner, and calls registry.deactivate_executor(&owner), with a new InvalidEventData error for malformed logs. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: ExecutorDeactivated logs with an indexed owner topic must deactivate that owner in the executor registry, and logs missing the owner topic must be rejected as invalid event data.
suggested_falsification_tests: Register an executor in the in-memory registry, feed an ExecutorDeactivated log whose topic[1] is that address, and assert is_executor_active returns false; feed the same signature with only topic[0] and assert InvalidEventData and unchanged registry state.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Feed an ExecutorDeactivated log with and without topic[1]; current code has no log handler that deactivates the owner or returns InvalidEventData.
title: Deactivate events mark owner inactive
